key_metrics: Gross Profit: $1.43 billion (Gross Margin compressed to 55.6% from 58.5% YoY)
Inventories: $2.00 billion (up from $1.44 billion at fiscal year-end)
qoq_changes: Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $1.04 billion from $1.98 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Inventories increased significantly to $2.00 billion from $1.44 billion at fiscal year-end.
The company executed $190.9 million in share repurchases during the quarter.
new_risks: Inventory buildup risk: Inventories surged to $2.00 billion, which may necessitate future markdowns if sell-through slows.
Margin compression: Cost of goods sold (+14.6% YoY) and SG&A expenses (+8.6% YoY) outpaced revenue growth (+7.1% YoY), negatively impacting operating income.
management_tone: Cautious; while top-line revenue continues to grow, rising costs, gross margin compression, and a heavy inventory build are weighing on bottom-line profitability.
analyst_note: Lululemon posted a mixed Q3 2025, achieving 7.1% YoY revenue growth but suffering a 12.8% drop in net income due to rising costs and margin contraction. The notable inventory buildup to $2.0 billion and declining operating income are key areas of concern heading into the critical holiday season.
